results.title: Limitation of Liability
results._source_text_exact: 5.1 Provider's aggregate liability for a Security Incident caused by Provider's failure to meet the service levels in Exhibit A shall not exceed three (3) times the annual fees paid by Contracting Party A, and shall be uncapped in the event of Provider's gross negligence or willful misconduct.
results.original_text: 5.1 Provider's aggregate liability for a Security Incident caused by Provider's failure to meet the service levels in Exhibit A shall not exceed three (3) times the annual fees paid by Contracting Party A, and shall be uncapped in the event of Provider's gross negligence or willful misconduct.
results.reasoning_evidence: 5.1 Provider's aggregate liability for a Security Incident caused by Provider's failure to meet the service levels in Exhibit A shall not exceed three (3) times
results.quoted_text: Provider's aggregate liability for a Security Incident caused by Provider's failure to meet the service levels in Exhibit A shall not exceed three (3) times the annual fees paid by Contracting Party A, and shall be uncapped in the event of Provider's gross negligence or willful misconduct.
results.explanation_simple: The clause limits Provider's liability for Security Incidents but allows for unlimited liability in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct.
results.why_it_matters: This clause creates a significant financial exposure for the Provider in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct, as there is no cap on liability in those scenarios.
results.legal_insight: Manual review required: generated text did not pass source-fidelity validation.
results.market_comparison: This clause is somewhat aggressive as it allows for uncapped liability in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct, which is not typical in many commercial agreements.
results.red_flag_reason: The clause allows for unlimited liability in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct, creating significant financial exposure for the Provider.
results.recommendation: Check whether the liability cap, exclusions, and carve-outs are clearly stated and proportionate.
results.safer_alternative: Provider's aggregate liability for a Security Incident shall not exceed the total annual fees paid by Contracting Party A, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct, where liability shall be capped at three (3) times the annual fees.
section_path: 5. LIABILITY AND INDEMNIFICATION

5.1 Provider's aggregate liability for a Security Incident caused by Provider's failure to meet the service levels in Exhibit A shall not exceed three (3) times the annual fees paid by Contracting Party A, and shall be uncapped in the event of Provider's gross negligence or willful misconduct.
risk_reasons: Liability allocation may affect financial exposure or available remedies.
Payment provisions may affect cash flow or performance rights.
Security obligations may require technical controls, incident response, monitoring, or audit readiness.
Service obligations should be assessed together with performance standards and remedies.

results.title: Termination Rights
results._source_text_exact: 6.1 This Agreement has an initial term of three (3) years. Either Party may terminate for the other's uncured material breach following thirty (30) days' written notice. Contracting Party A may terminate immediately, without penalty, if Provider suffers a Security Incident affecting Contracting Party A data caused by Provider's gross negligence.
results.original_text: 6.1 This Agreement has an initial term of three (3) years. Either Party may terminate for the other's uncured material breach following thirty (30) days' written notice. Contracting Party A may terminate immediately, without penalty, if Provider suffers a Security Incident affecting Contracting Party A data caused by Provider's gross negligence.
results.reasoning_evidence: 6.1 This Agreement has an initial term of three (3) years. Either Party may terminate for the other's uncured material breach following thirty (30) days' writte
results.quoted_text: Either Party may terminate for the other's uncured material breach following thirty (30) days' written notice. Contracting Party A may terminate immediately, without penalty, if Provider suffers a Security Incident affecting Contracting Party A data caused by Provider's gross negligence.
results.explanation_simple: The clause allows termination for material breach with notice and immediate termination for security incidents due to gross negligence.
results.why_it_matters: The ability to terminate for material breach provides a safeguard for both parties, while immediate termination for security incidents protects Contracting Party A's data.
results.market_comparison: The termination rights are relatively standard, but the immediate termination clause for security incidents may be seen as aggressive.
section_path: 6. TERM AND TERMINATION

6.1 This Agreement has an initial term of three (3) years. Either Party may terminate for the other's uncured material breach following thirty (30) days' written notice. Contracting Party A may terminate immediately, without penalty, if Provider suffers a Security Incident affecting Contracting Party A data caused by Provider's gross negligence.
risk_reasons: Termination provisions may affect operational continuity or legal remedies.
Security obligations may require technical controls, incident response, monitoring, or audit readiness.

results.title: On-Site Security Audit
results._source_text_exact: 3.2 Contracting Party A shall have the right, upon thirty (30) days' notice, to conduct an on-site security audit of Provider's facilities directly relevant to the Services, no more than once annually, at Contracting Party A's expense.
results.original_text: 3.2 Contracting Party A shall have the right, upon thirty (30) days' notice, to conduct an on-site security audit of Provider's facilities directly relevant to the Services, no more than once annually, at Contracting Party A's expense.
results.reasoning_evidence: 3.2 Contracting Party A shall have the right, upon thirty (30) days' notice, to conduct an on-site security audit of Provider's facilities directly relevant to the Services,
results.quoted_text: Contracting Party A shall have the right, upon thirty (30) days' notice, to conduct an on-site security audit of Provider's facilities directly relevant to the Services, no more than once annually, at Contracting Party A's expense.
results.explanation_simple: Contracting Party A can conduct an annual security audit with prior notice.
results.why_it_matters: This clause allows Contracting Party A to ensure compliance and security standards are met by the Provider.
results.legal_insight: This clause affects control, approvals, compliance duties, audit rights, assignment, or governance processes.
results.market_comparison: This clause is standard in contracts involving sensitive data or security considerations.
results.recommendation: Check approval rights, assignment limits, audit scope, compliance obligations, subcontracting controls, and consequences of breach.
results.negotiation_advice: Consider defining notice methods, required addresses, deemed receipt, electronic delivery, language requirements, emergency notices, and change-of-address procedures.
section_path: 3. COMPLIANCE AND AUDIT

3.2 Contracting Party A shall have the right, upon thirty (30) days' notice, to conduct an on-site security audit of Provider's facilities directly relevant to the Services, no more than once annually, at Contracting Party A's expense.

results.title: Notification of Security Incidents and Vulnerabilities
results._source_text_exact: 2.1 Provider shall notify Contracting Party A of any confirmed Security Incident within four (4) hours of confirmation, and of any Critical Vulnerability within twenty-four (24) hours of discovery.
results.original_text: 2.1 Provider shall notify Contracting Party A of any confirmed Security Incident within four (4) hours of confirmation, and of any Critical Vulnerability within twenty-four (24) hours of discovery.
results.reasoning_evidence: 2.1 Provider shall notify Contracting Party A of any confirmed Security Incident within four (4) hours of confirmation, and of any Critical Vulnerability within twenty-four
results.clause_title: Notification of Security Incidents and Vulnerabilities
results.quoted_text: Provider shall notify Contracting Party A of any confirmed Security Incident within four (4) hours of confirmation, and of any Critical Vulnerability within twenty-four (24) hours of discovery.
results.explanation_simple: The Provider is required to notify Contracting Party A of security incidents and vulnerabilities within specified timeframes.
results.why_it_matters: Timely notification allows Contracting Party A to respond quickly to potential threats, reducing the risk of data breaches or other security issues.
results.legal_insight: The clause addresses incident or breach handling, so timing, cooperation, evidence preservation, notification duties, and liability treatment should be clear.
results.market_comparison: The requirement for prompt notification is common in security agreements, but the specific timeframes may vary.
results.recommendation: Check processing instructions, security standards, subprocessor controls, breach notification timing, audit rights, deletion or return duties, and regulatory allocation.
section_path: 2. INCIDENT RESPONSE AND NOTIFICATION

results.title: Indemnification for Security Incident

results.quoted_text: Provider shall indemnify Contracting Party A for third-party claims, regulatory fines, and reasonable costs of credit monitoring and forensic investigation arising from a Security Incident attributable to Provider's breach of this Agreement.

clauses.title: Indemnification for Security Incident

clauses.quoted_text: Provider shall indemnify Contracting Party A for third-party claims, regulatory fines, and reasonable costs of credit monitoring and forensic investigation arising from a Security Incident attributable to Provider's breach of this Agreement.

clauses.title: Limitation of Liability
clauses._source_text_exact: 5.1 Provider's aggregate liability for a Security Incident caused by Provider's failure to meet the service levels in Exhibit A shall not exceed three (3) times the annual fees paid by Contracting Party A, and shall be uncapped in the event of Provider's gross negligence or willful misconduct.
clauses.original_text: 5.1 Provider's aggregate liability for a Security Incident caused by Provider's failure to meet the service levels in Exhibit A shall not exceed three (3) times the annual fees paid by Contracting Party A, and shall be uncapped in the event of Provider's gross negligence or willful misconduct.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 5.1 Provider's aggregate liability for a Security Incident caused by Provider's failure to meet the service levels in Exhibit A shall not exceed three (3) times
clauses.quoted_text: Provider's aggregate liability for a Security Incident caused by Provider's failure to meet the service levels in Exhibit A shall not exceed three (3) times the annual fees paid by Contracting Party A, and shall be uncapped in the event of Provider's gross negligence or willful misconduct.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause limits Provider's liability for Security Incidents but allows for unlimited liability in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct.
clauses.why_it_matters: This clause creates a significant financial exposure for the Provider in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct, as there is no cap on liability in those scenarios.
clauses.legal_insight: Manual review required: generated text did not pass source-fidelity validation.
clauses.market_comparison: This clause is somewhat aggressive as it allows for uncapped liability in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct, which is not typical in many commercial agreements.
clauses.red_flag_reason: The clause allows for unlimited liability in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct, creating significant financial exposure for the Provider.
clauses.recommendation: Check whether the liability cap, exclusions, and carve-outs are clearly stated and proportionate.
clauses.safer_alternative: Provider's aggregate liability for a Security Incident shall not exceed the total annual fees paid by Contracting Party A, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct, where liability shall be capped at three (3) times the annual fees.
section_path: 5. LIABILITY AND INDEMNIFICATION

5.1 Provider's aggregate liability for a Security Incident caused by Provider's failure to meet the service levels in Exhibit A shall not exceed three (3) times the annual fees paid by Contracting Party A, and shall be uncapped in the event of Provider's gross negligence or willful misconduct.
risk_reasons: Liability allocation may affect financial exposure or available remedies.

clauses.title: Termination Rights
clauses._source_text_exact: 6.1 This Agreement has an initial term of three (3) years. Either Party may terminate for the other's uncured material breach following thirty (30) days' written notice. Contracting Party A may terminate immediately, without penalty, if Provider suffers a Security Incident affecting Contracting Party A data caused by Provider's gross negligence.
clauses.original_text: 6.1 This Agreement has an initial term of three (3) years. Either Party may terminate for the other's uncured material breach following thirty (30) days' written notice. Contracting Party A may terminate immediately, without penalty, if Provider suffers a Security Incident affecting Contracting Party A data caused by Provider's gross negligence.

clauses.quoted_text: Either Party may terminate for the other's uncured material breach following thirty (30) days' written notice. Contracting Party A may terminate immediately, without penalty, if Provider suffers a Security Incident affecting Contracting Party A data caused by Provider's gross negligence.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause allows termination for material breach with notice and immediate termination for security incidents due to gross negligence.
clauses.why_it_matters: The ability to terminate for material breach provides a safeguard for both parties, while immediate termination for security incidents protects Contracting Party A's data.
clauses.market_comparison: The termination rights are relatively standard, but the immediate termination clause for security incidents may be seen as aggressive.
section_path: 6. TERM AND TERMINATION

6.1 This Agreement has an initial term of three (3) years. Either Party may terminate for the other's uncured material breach following thirty (30) days' written notice. Contracting Party A may terminate immediately, without penalty, if Provider suffers a Security Incident affecting Contracting Party A data caused by Provider's gross negligence.
risk_reasons: Termination provisions may affect operational continuity or legal remedies.
Security obligations may require technical controls, incident response, monitoring, or audit readiness.

clauses.original_text: 1.1 Provider shall deliver 24x7x365 security monitoring, threat detection, and incident response services as further described in Exhibit A, consistent with the NIST Cybersecurity Framework.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 1.1 Provider shall deliver 24x7x365 security monitoring, threat detection, and incident response services as further described in Exhibit A, consistent with the
clauses.quoted_text: Provider shall deliver 24x7x365 security monitoring, threat detection, and incident response services as further described in Exhibit A, consistent with the NIST Cybersecurity Framework.
clauses.explanation_simple: The Provider is obligated to deliver continuous security services.
clauses.why_it_matters: This clause ensures that security monitoring and incident response are consistently available, which is critical for protecting against cyber threats.
clauses.legal_insight: This clause governs data handling, privacy obligations, security controls, or incident response responsibilities.
clauses.market_comparison: This level of service is generally considered standard in cybersecurity agreements.
clauses.recommendation: Check processing instructions, security standards, subprocessor controls, breach notification timing, audit rights, deletion or return duties, and regulatory allocation.
section_path: 1. SCOPE OF SERVICES
1.1 Provider shall deliver 24x7x365 security monitoring, threat detection, and incident response services as further described in Exhibit A, consistent with the NIST Cybersecurity Framework.

clauses.title: Indemnification for Security Incident
clauses._source_text_exact: 5.2 Provider shall indemnify Contracting Party A for third-party claims, regulatory fines, and reasonable costs of credit monitoring and forensic investigation arising from a Security Incident attributable to Provider's breach of this Agreement.
clauses.original_text: 5.2 Provider shall indemnify Contracting Party A for third-party claims, regulatory fines, and reasonable costs of credit monitoring and forensic investigation arising from a Security Incident attributable to Provider's breach of this Agreement.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 5.2 Provider shall indemnify Contracting Party A for third-party claims, regulatory fines, and reasonable costs of credit monitoring and forensic investigation arising from
clauses.quoted_text: Provider shall indemnify Contracting Party A for third-party claims, regulatory fines, and reasonable costs of credit monitoring and forensic investigation arising from a Security Incident attributable to Provider's breach of this Agreement.
clauses.explanation_simple: The Provider is responsible for indemnifying Contracting Party A against certain costs and claims related to Security Incidents.
clauses.why_it_matters: This clause places financial responsibility on the Provider for specific incidents, which could lead to significant costs depending on the nature of the Security Incident.
clauses.legal_insight: The clause addresses incident or breach handling, so timing, cooperation, evidence preservation, notification duties, and liability treatment should be clear.
clauses.market_comparison: Indemnification clauses for security incidents are common, but the extent of coverage can vary significantly.
clauses.recommendation: Check processing instructions, security standards, subprocessor controls, breach notification timing, audit rights, deletion or return duties, and regulatory allocation.
clauses.safer_alternative: Provider shall indemnify Contracting Party A for third-party claims, regulatory fines, and reasonable costs of credit monitoring and forensic investigation arising from a Security Incident attributable to Provider's breach of this Agreement, up to a maximum of [insert cap amount].
section_path: 5. LIABILITY AND INDEMNIFICATION

5.2 Provider shall indemnify Contracting Party A for third-party claims, regulatory fines, and reasonable costs of credit monitoring and forensic investigation arising from a Security Incident attributable to Provider's breach of this Agreement.
risk_reasons: Security obligations may require technical controls, incident response, monitoring, or audit readiness.

clauses.title: Subcontractor Engagement
clauses._source_text_exact: 4.1 Provider shall not engage a new subcontractor with access to Contracting Party A systems or data without Contracting Party A's prior written approval, which shall not be unreasonably withheld.
clauses.original_text: 4.1 Provider shall not engage a new subcontractor with access to Contracting Party A systems or data without Contracting Party A's prior written approval, which shall not be unreasonably withheld.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 4.1 Provider shall not engage a new subcontractor with access to Contracting Party A systems or data without Contracting Party A's prior written approval, which shall not be unreasonably
clauses.quoted_text: Provider shall not engage a new subcontractor with access to Contracting Party A systems or data without Contracting Party A's prior written approval, which shall not be unreasonably withheld.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause requires the Provider to obtain written approval from Contracting Party A before engaging new subcontractors with access to Contracting Party A systems or data.
clauses.why_it_matters: This clause protects Contracting Party A's data and systems by ensuring they have control over who can access them.
clauses.legal_insight: This clause affects control, approvals, compliance duties, audit rights, assignment, or governance processes.
clauses.market_comparison: This clause is common in contracts involving sensitive data or systems, as it ensures that the primary party retains control over access to their resources.
clauses.recommendation: Check approval rights, assignment limits, audit scope, compliance obligations, subcontracting controls, and consequences of breach.
section_path: 4. SUBCONTRACTORS AND SUBPROCESSORS
4.1 Provider shall not engage a new subcontractor with access to Contracting Party A systems or data without Contracting Party A's prior written approval, which shall not be unreasonably withheld.
